speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Bahar se wapas aakar yahan ke restricted mahaul mein mera dum ghut-ta hai. Par mummy-papa ko bura na lage, isliye dost ki shaadi miss hone par maine thakan ka bahana kiya. Main nahi chahti ki unhe lage ki unki beti NRI lifestyle ki wajah se bigad gayi hai. Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon aur apna akelepan chhupati hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational element of protecting parents' feelings over her own.
text: Tum apne parents ko hurt nahi karna chahti, isliye unki feelings aur ghar ka mahaul theek rakhne ke liye tum apne dum ghutne waale ehsaas ko unse chhupa rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Haan... I mean, agar maine sach bola toh unko lagega ki main unke saath khush nahi hoon. Woh mujhe ungrateful samjhenge, aur society bhi bolegi ki bahar ja kar ladki kharab ho gayi hai. Isliye better hai chup raho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the feared consequence of being judged by family and society.
text: Tumhe is baat ka darr lag raha hai ki tumhara sach unko disappoint kar dega aur log tumhe galat samajh lenge...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Exactly. Mere liye adjust karna already itna hard hai, but if I complain, it becomes a bigger issue. Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon, even though it feels very suffocating inside.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask an open-ended question based on her feeling of suffocation.
text: Yeh jo ander ek suffocation si feel hoti hai tumhe akele mein, woh kaisa lagta hai us waqt?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Aisa lagta hai jaise main apne hi ghar mein mehmaan hoon. I have to think twice before doing anything. Kapde se le kar bahar jaane ke time tak, sab kuch filter karna padta hai taaki koi kalesh na ho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the duty frame of constantly filtering oneself for others.
text: Aise lagta hai ki khud ko ek ache bache ki tarah pesh karna tumhari ek roz ki duty ban gayi hai, jisme tumhara apna astitva kahin chhip sa gaya hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: True. Ek ajeeb sa loneliness hai. Main physically yahan hoon but mentally wapas jaana chahti hoon. But unka ek lauta bachcha hoon, kaise chhod dun? I am stuck.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Explore the feeling of being stuck between two lives.
text: Jab yeh khayal aata hai ki tum stuck ho gayi ho apne decisions aur family ki expectations ke beech, toh tum khud ko kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Main bas ignore karti hoon. Room mein band ho kar thoda rone ka mann karta hai, par wahan bhi I have to be careful. Meri mom turant puchhengi kya hua. It's draining.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time/duration of this continuous exhausting performance.
text: Yeh sab din-raat continuously chal raha hai aur is ek constant pehredari ne tumhe bohot zyada thaka diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Haa, thak gayi hoon acting kar kar ke. Kisi ko mera real self nahi dikh raha ab. Main sochti hoon ki shayad time ke saath main hi adjust ho jaungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask what losing her real self feels like.
text: Tumne kaha ki tumhara real self ab kisi ko nahi dikh raha, is baat ka tumhare andar kaisa asar pad raha hai?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Aisa lagta hai ki main exist hi nahi karti. Jo ladki bahar itni confident aur independent thi, woh yahan aakar bilkul gumm ho gayi hai family ki needs aur izzat ke peeche.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the contrast between her independent identity and present helplessness.
text: Ek darr sa baith gaya hai ki tumhari purani independent identity in sab umeedon aur parivaar ki boundaries mein kahin poori tarah na kho jaaye.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Haan bilkul. It's like I have no choice left. Agar main apni life choose karti hoon toh main selfish, aur agar unki life choose karti hoon toh main andar se zero ho jati hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feeling of being trapped (TRAPPED/HELPLESS).
text: Lagta hai jaise dono hi raaston par tumhari haar hai, aur is situation mein tum khud ko poori tarah se helpless aur fasa hua mehsoos kar rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Yes. Fasa hua is the right word. Meri apni friends jo bahar hain unki life dekh kar I feel so left behind. Yahan main bas 'achi beti' banne mein time waste kar rahi hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her struggle as a common structural issue for young NRI returnees, separating worth from outcome.
text: Tumhara yeh alienation aur fasa hua mehsoos karna bilkul natural hai; jab koi return karta hai ek metro environment mein jahan family izzat ka pressure zyada ho, toh adjust karna bahut exhausting hota hai, aur isme tumhari koi galti nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: I guess so. Thanks for saying that. Kabhi kabhi I wonder if I should just explode and tell them everything. But then mummy ko BP hai, I can't take that risk.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Explore what holds her back beyond the medical risk.
text: Is darr ke alawa ki unki health par asar padega, aur kya aisi cheez hai jo tumhe apne man ki baat kehne se rok rahi hai?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Unka woh disappointed face. They did a lot for me, mujhe padhne bahar bheja, despite rishtedaars talking. Unki mehnat ko I don't want to throw in their face by saying I am miserable with them.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share an anecdote from a similar young professional world.
text: Young professionals mein yeh apne parents ki qurbaniyon ka bojh khud akele uthana bahut common hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki society lagatar yaad dilati hai ki humein humesha shukraguzar rehna chahiye.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Exactly this. I am very grateful. It's just that meri personal space bilkul khatam ho chuki hai. Is shukraguzari ne mujhe pinjare mein daal diya hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the paradox of gratitude and entrapment.
text: Lagta hai tumhe is baat se takleef hai ki jis ehsaan aur pyaar ke liye tum shukraguzar ho, wahi ab tumhare liye ek ghutan bhari jail ban gaya hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Yes, jail is the exact feeling. It's so frustrating. Kabhi lagta hai apna phone chhod kar kahin gayab ho jaun taaki temporarily is akelepan aur guilt se chhutkara mile.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate the urge to escape as a normal response to high pressure.
text: Jab charo taraf se itna guilt aur pressure aata hai parivaar ki ummeedon ka, tab aise gayab hone ke khayal aana bohot normal hai; tum kuch galat nahi soch rahi.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: I feel slightly better hearing that. Maybe I am not going completely crazy. Par yeh chalega kaise long term? I can't keep faking it forever.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Move towards what she envisions as a manageable balance.
text: Agar hum thoda chhota sochein, toh tumhare hisaab se ek chhota sa badlaav kya ho sakta hai jisse thodi saans lene ki jagah mil sake bina kisi kalesh ke?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: I don't know. Shayad main din mein kuch waqt akele spend kar sakti hoon, lekin bina unhe bura feel karaye. Ab batao, kya karoon main iske liye?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide small, low-conflict options that give her private time.
text: Hum do choti cheezein try kar sakte hain. Ya toh tum apne kisi professional kaam ya course ka naam le kar din mein 45 minutes door lock karke baith sakti ho, ya phir subah jaldi uth kar sirf apne liye ek cup chai akele balcony mein pi sakti ho. Inme se kaun sa tumhare routine mein aasaan lagega?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Subah waala idea theek lag raha hai. Mummy papa late uthte hain, toh us time I won't have to explain anything to them or make excuses.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframe taking space as essential, not selfish.
text: Aisi chhoti spaces nikalna humare jaise samaj mein bahut zaruri hota hai jahan boundaries samajh nahi aati; yeh selfish nahi hai, balki lambe samay tak apni sanity maintain karne ka tareeqa hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Sanity maintain karna hi mera biggest challenge hai abhi. Par I will try this morning routine. Kam se kam main wahan akele earphones laga ke apna music toh sun sakti hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk through the small morning routine step by step.
text: Pehle kal subah apne time par uthna... phir ek chai banana aur apni pasand ka music lagana earphones par... bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Sounds doable. Agar mom pooche bhi toh bol dungi ki aise hi fresh hawa khaane uthi thi. I won't have to carry the burden of their expectations for those 20 minutes.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her practical approach and boundaries.
text: Tumhari yeh chhoti si tarkeeb bilkul sahi aur practical hai, jisse tum apni privacy maintain kar logi aur parents ko bura bhi nahi lagega.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ghar ki shanti aur izzat bachane ke liye bas chup rahti hoon
text: Thank you. Aaj pehli baar kisi se ye clear bol ke thoda halka lag raha hai. I guess mujhe lag raha tha I am a bad daughter for feeling suffocated.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Close with a normalizing reframe.
text: Ek achhi beti hone ka matlab khud ko poori tarah mita dena nahi hota, aur yeh badlaav dheere dheere aayega tumhari apni choti koshishon se.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Dadi ki caregiving mein poori raat nikal gayi aur main shaadi mein nahi ja saki, par asli wajah wo culture shock hai jisne mujhe gher rakha hai. Main apni struggle kisi ko nahi batati kyunki biradari mein log kahenge ki NRI hokar nakhre kar rahi hai, aur main Papa ki 'shaan' mein koi kami nahi aane dena chahti. Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon, bhale hi andar se main bilkul misfit mehsus karun.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational element of protecting the father's image.
text: Apne papa ki shaan aur ghar ki shanti banaye rakhne ke liye, tum andar hi andar is misfit waale ehsaas ko akele hi utha rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Haan. I mean, biradari mein kisi ko samajh nahi aayega mera culture shock. They will just say I have an attitude problem and I am ashamed of our roots.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feared consequence softly in response to the stigma cue.
text: Log tumhare struggle ko 'NRI nakhre' samajh lenge, yeh darr tumhe sach bolne se rokta hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Exactly. To be honest, mere paas aur koi option bhi nahi hai. Mujhe adjust karna hi padega. Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon, par sach kahun toh raat mein akele rona aata hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about how she manages the burden when she is alone.
text: Jab raat mein sabke so jaane ke baad akelepan ka yeh bojh tum par aata hai, tab tum khud ko kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: I just scroll on my phone. Puraani photos dekhti hoon jab main bahar thi. Wahan meri ek life thi, I had my own space. Yahan meri identity sirf dadi ki caregiver aur Papa ki 'achi beti' tak simat gayi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the role/duty frame and loss of identity.
text: Ek azaad insaan se sirf ek achi beti aur caregiver ki bhumika mein khud ko dhaalna, tumhari poori pehchaan ko hi badal raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Haan. It feels like my past life was a dream. Main ab waisi nahi rahi. Lekin kabhi kabhi frustration itni badh jaati hai ki lagta hai bhag jaun yahan se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time/duration of repressing her desires leading to an urge to escape.
text: Itne lambe samay tak lagatar compromise karne ke baad, jab yeh thakan bahar aati hai toh aisi ghutan hoti hai jahan se bas nikal jaane ka man karta hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Sahi kaha. Lekin fir next second guilt aa jata hai ki they spent so much on my education. Main unko disappoint nahi kar sakti. Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon, but I feel totally trapped.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feeling of being trapped and helpless.
text: Lagta hai jaise in dono zindagi ke beech faste hue, tum khud ko poori tarah se akela aur helpless mehsoos kar rahi ho, jahan tumhara dard kisi ko dikh nahi raha.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: That is so true. Kisi ko nahi dikhta. Mummy Papa sochte hain ki I am adjusting well and I am so sanskari. But andar se mera dum ghut raha hai. I don't belong here anymore.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her struggle with acculturation as natural, separating it from personal failure.
text: Tumhara yeh isolation feel karna bilkul natural hai; jab ek NRI wapas apne society aur biradari mein aata hai, toh yeh shift bohot bada hota hai aur isme tumhare 'nakhre' nahi hain, balki ek genuine struggle hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Thank you for saying that. Mujhe lagta tha mujhme hi koi kami hai. But honestly, it's the lack of personal space and constant judgment from relatives that is killing my spirit.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share an anecdote normalizing culture shock for returning young women in Indian society.
text: Bahar se waapas aane waali young auraton mein yeh culture shock aur space kho dene ka ehsaas bohot common hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki humari society mein parivaar ki izzat pehle aati hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Bilkul. Ek baar maine try kiya tha bolne ka ki mujhe therapy ya counseling ki zaroorat ho sakti hai adjustment ke liye. Papa ka chehra dekh kar maine turant topic change kar diya.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Softly mirror the feared consequence due to the stigma cue.
text: Unki disappointment dekhne ka darr tumhe apni zarurat poori karne se rok deta hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Yes, I can't hurt them. Main afford bhi nahi kar sakti kisi aur ko batana kyunki sab relate karte hain papa ke naam se. I just need some way to survive this without losing myself completely. Ab kya karoon?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide 2 low-friction options for finding personal space without alarming family.
text: Hum kuch aisi chhoti cheezein soch sakte hain jo kisi ko pata bhi na chale aur tumhe apni thodi space mil jaaye. Tum din mein 15 minute ke liye apne room mein phone notes par apne thoughts likh sakti ho, ya fir dadi ke sone ke waqt kisi purane dost se message par baat kar sakti ho. In dono mein se kya aasaan lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Writing sounds better. Dadi dopahar mein soti hain. Main apne phone ke notes app mein likh sakti hoon so no one will even know I am journaling.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk through the process of private journaling step by step.
text: Pehle kal dopahar dadi ke sote hi apne room mein aana… phir apna notes app khol kar bas jo man mein aaye likhna… bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: I think I can do that. At least I won't have to filter my words there. Yahan toh har waqt bolne se pehle sochna padta hai ki log kya kahenge.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframe taking private time as essential for managing cultural pressure.
text: Ek naye mahaul mein jahan parivaar ki shaan ka itna pressure ho, wahan aisi ek private jagah banana apne dimaag ko thoda aaram dene ka ek zaroori hissa hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Haan. Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon, par kam se kam notes app mein toh main apna sach bol sakti hoon. It feels like a safe idea.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Explore what she might express first in her safe space.
text: Jab tum us notes app mein apna sach bina kisi filter ke likhogi, toh tumhe sabse pehle kya express karne ka man karega?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Shayad main apne purane friends ko address karke kuch likhungi. Jaise main unse baat kar rahi hoon. It might make me feel less lonely.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational need to bridge the gap with her past life.
text: Apne doston se ek virtual connection banane se tumhari us duniya se ek doori thodi kam ho jayegi, jo tum miss karti ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Yes. I miss them so much. Woh log mujhe bina kisi judgment ke samajhte thay. Yahan toh sabke dimaag mein ek set image hai meri, jise todna allowed nahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the duty frame of living up to a set image.
text: Logo ki us set image aur unki expectations ke mutabiq jeene mein tumhari original azaadi kahin poori tarah dab si gayi hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Exactly. I just wish someone here would see how hard I am trying. Papa ko lagta hai it's very easy for me to just slip back into this lifestyle.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the lack of recognition causing pain.
text: Lagta hai tumhe is baat se takleef hoti hai ki tumhari itni mehnat aur qurbani ko koi recognize nahi kar raha, bas use ek sadharan farz maan liya gaya hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: That's exactly it. 'Farz' bol kar sab normalise kar diya. Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon, aur woh meri muskurahat ko hi mera poora sach maan lete hain.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask how she plans to sustain herself amidst this 'farz'.
text: Is situation mein, jab sab kuch farz ke naam par normal maan liya jata hai, tumhare andar jo thakan hoti hai, use tum kin choti cheezon se halka karne ka soch rahi ho?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Bas wahi akele baithna. Aur ab jo aapne bola, phone notes mein likhna bina kisi dar ke. Usse mujhe shayad thoda genuine relief mile.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her maturity and efforts.
text: Tum jis maturity ke saath apne family ki izzat aur apne dard ko balance kar rahi ho, woh waqai bohot mushkil kaam hai, aur tum khud ke liye jo yeh chote steps le rahi ho, woh bahut mayne rakhte hain.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: Thank you. Ye sun kar sach mein thoda strength milta hai. I guess I have to take it one day at a time. I can't change their mindset overnight.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reiterate that structural shifts take time, but creating boundaries is possible now.
text: Biradari aur samaj ki purani soch ko badalna aasaan nahi hota, par uske beech apne liye ek chhoti si safe boundary banana zaroor tumhare haath mein hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Parivaar ki harmony ke liye main bas muskurati hoon
text: You are right. Safe boundary is key right now. Main aaj dopahar se hi start karungi likhna. Thank you for listening without calling me a spoiled NRI.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: End on a warm restatement of her readiness.
text: Mujhe khushi hai ki tum is naye plan ko try karne ka hausla juta paayi, aur bina kisi dar ke apni sachhi baatein yahan saajha kar saki.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon
text: Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon. Yahan ki safety concerns aur bhasha ka badlav mujhe bilkul pardesi mehsus karata hai, par shaadi miss hone par maine chup-chap saas-bahu wale kalesh se bachne ke liye thakan ka naam liya. Agar maine bataya ki main yahan khush nahi hoon, toh Mummy-Papa ko lagega ki unki itni saalon ki mehnat bekaar gayi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational element of protecting parents' pride and avoiding household conflict.
text: Tum apne Mummy-Papa ki mehnat aur sasural ke mahaul ko bachaane ke chakkar mein, apne andar chal rahi is ghutan ko akele hi daba rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, kyunki agar maine complain ki toh ghar mein kalesh shuru ho jayega. Meri saas pehle hi taane marti hain mere kaam aur naye shehar ke adjustment ko lekar. Office aur ghar ka dual-burden it's so exhausting.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the feared consequence of her struggle turning into a larger household issue.
text: Is naye mahual mein ek darr sa laga rehta hai ki tumhari ek chhoti si shikaayat bhi ghar mein bada mudda ban sakti hai aur tumhare parents ka garv toot sakta hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon
text: Exactly. Maine office ka commute aur domestic work manage karne ki koshish ki, but the language barrier is so real. I feel like an alien here. Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon, par sach me theek nahi hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about the feeling of being an alien/pardesi during her daily routine.
text: Jab tum office ki is bhaag-daud aur language barrier ke beech khud ko itna akele aur 'alien' jaisa mehsoos karti ho, tab tum is feeling ko kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Sambhalna kya, main bas chup rehti hoon. Yahan meri koi support system nahi hai. Agar apne colleagues ya relatives ko bataungi toh wo mujhe backward ya weak samjhenge, jo naye mahaul mein adjust nahi kar pa rahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Softly mirror the time/duration of carrying this stigma alone.
text: Logon ki in nazron aur judgment ne tumhe itne dino se apni baatein andar rakhne par majboor kar diya hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes. I am constantly walking on eggshells. Ek taraf meeting ka stress, doosri taraf saas ki endless expectations. Mere parents ne itna padhaya-likhaya, so they think I am living a successful life. But sach to ye hai ki main trapped hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the core emotion of feeling trapped/helpless due to relational preservation and category constraints.
text: Lagta hai in alag-alag duniya aur ummeedon ke beech faste hue tum bilkul trapped aur helpless mehsoos kar rahi ho, jahan koi tumhara sach dekhne wala nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi kaha. Kabhi-kabhi lagta hai meri apni identity khatam ho gayi hai. Main bas ek obedient bahu aur ek employee banke reh gayi hoon jisko apni thakan ya darr bolne ka haq nahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her struggle with acculturation and dual-burden as natural.
text: Tumhara yeh pardesi aur disconnected feel karna bilkul normal hai; jab koi naye shehar mein safety issues face karta hai aur uspar ghar-office ka double pressure ho, toh apni identity khona ek aam baat hai, aur isme tumhari koi kami nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you. It feels somewhat good ki koi toh mujhe samajh raha hai. Mere husband bhi busy rehte hain aur unhe lagta hai ki main choti baaton pe overthink kar rahi hoon. I just don't want to create more drama.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a systemic anecdote validating her dual-burden experience.
text: Working auraton mein yeh naye environment aur saas-bahu ke kalesh ka dual-burden akele uthana bahut common hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki humari society mein ghar ki maryada sabse pehle aati hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Wahi maryada ki wajah se I am losing my mind. Main literally commute karte waqt metro mein rone lagti hoon chhup kar. Mujhe kuch samajh nahi aa raha aage ka, main is akelepan mein theek kaise rahoon?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offer small, actionable suggestions that fit her commute and dual-burden routine.
text: Hum ek-do chhote steps try kar sakte hain jo tumhare is tight routine mein fit ho jayein. Jaise metro commute ke waqt apne earphones par apni native bhasha ka koi music ya podcast sunna, ya phir weekend par kisi aisi purani dost ko 10 minute call karna jisse koi expectation na ho. Inme se kya doable lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Commute ke time music/podcast sunne wala theek hai. Metro me I usually just overthink about office meetings and ghar ka kaam. Native language ka kuch sun lungi toh maybe thoda apne ghar jaisa feel hoga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframe taking space during commute as a structural necessity.
text: Aise chhote-chhote moments nikalna apni pehchaan ko zinda rakhne ke liye zaruri hai; yeh koi aalsi pan nahi balki ek naye aur demanding system mein survive karne ka bada tareeqa hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon
text: I never thought of it like that. Mujhe hamesha lagta tha agar main thoda relax karungi toh main apni duties se bhaag rahi hoon. Bas unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon. It became a habit.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk through the small commute routine step by step.
text: Toh kal office jaate waqt pehle apne phone mein apne gaon ya shehar ki bhasha ka koi favourite gaana download karna... aur earphones lagakar bas use sunna... aaj bas itna hi koshish karna apne safar ke dauran.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, main ek apni playlist bana sakti hoon. At least headphones lagake commute karungi toh log ghoorenge nahi aur baat nahi karenge. Yahan ajeeb logo se safety ka darr waise bhi har roz rehta hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her finding a way to cope with the safety fears.
text: Tumhara headphones ke zariye us unsafe aur anjaan environment se khud ko block karna tumhare andar ke darr ko thoda shant karne ka ek asardar tareeqa ban raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi mein. Pata hai, main actually apni mummy ko bhi bahut miss karti hoon is naye shehar mein, par main unhe ye tension nahi de sakti. They live in a small town, unhe lagta hai ki metro city me meri life set hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about how she maintains connection while protecting them.
text: Jab tum apni mummy ko apne in saare struggles se door rakhne ki koshish karti ho, toh us waqt unse baat karte hue apne us pyar ko kaise balance karti ho bina tension diye?

speaker: Seeker
text: Main bas unse upar-upar se baat karti hoon. Khaana kha liya, mausam kaisa hai... I avoid talking about work deadlines or my saas ke taane. But usse ek distance aa gaya hai humare beech.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational distance created by trying to protect them.
text: Aisa lagta hai ki unhe takleef se bachane ke chakkkar mein, baaton mein ek aisi emotional deewar khadi ho gayi hai jisne tumhe apne hi parents se aur bhi door kar diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes, absolutely. I am basically acting like a robot every single day. Subah utho, sabke liye nashta banao, office bhago, then wapas aake fir domestic work shuru. And then smile like everything is perfect.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the duty frame of her mechanical daily routine.
text: Tumhara poora din un duties ko fulfill karne ki ek mechanical performance ban kar reh gaya hai, jisme tumhare apne aaram ki koi jagah nahi bachi.

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. Even though I am earning well, mentally main bankrupt feel karti hoon. Agar office me thodi peace milti toh shayad asaan hota, par yahan ki local bhasha bilkul alag hai, mujhe meeting me apni baat bolne mein bhi ajeeb lagta hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalize the language gap as a systemic issue, not a personal flaw.
text: Nayi bhasha aur naye work culture ka yeh gap aksar humare confidence ko bohot gehri thes pahunchata hai, aur yeh kisi professional failure ka nahi balki environment ke naye hone ka nateeja hota hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: That makes a lot of sense. I keep blaming myself ki I am not smart enough to fit in here. Par shayad the environment itself is intimidating. I need to stop being so harsh on myself all the time.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Explore moments of relief from this self-blame.
text: Kya koi aisa chhota sa moment hota hai din bhar mein jahan tum apne aap ko is saare self-blame aur double duty ke bojh se thoda azaad mehsoos karti ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad raat ko sone se pehle jab main 5-10 minute balcony me akeli khadi hoti hoon. Wahan koi kaam nahi hota, koi expectation nahi hoti. Bas thandi hawa hoti hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time/duration of this small space of relief.
text: Wo balcony ke 5-10 minute tumhare liye us ghutan bhare din mein ek iklauti saans lene ki jagah ki tarah kaam karte hain, jahan tum sach me khud ke saath hoti ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon
text: Yes. Unhi 10 minute me main kabhi kabhi chupke se ro leti hoon. Unke garv aur ghar ki maryada ke liye main apne saare aansu pee kar bas 'theek hoon' kehti hoon sabke saamne, par wahan koi dekhne wala nahi hota.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the absence of fear in her safe space.
text: Dekh kar lagta hai ki woh jagah sach much ek safe thikana ban gayi hai jahan kisi kalesh ya maryada tootne ka koi darr nahi hota aur tum apne aansuon ko behne ki ijazat de paati ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan. Main wahan bilkul akeli aur azaad hoti hoon. Ab se main earphones wali baat zarur try karungi metro me. It will give me another small safe space in this big alien city.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her proactive step to recreate that safe space.
text: Yeh ek naya safe space dhoondhne ka faisla tumhari ek bohot pyari koshish hai, jisse tum apni us azaadi ko sirf raat tak mehdood na rakh kar safar mein bhi mehsoos karogi.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you so much. Mujhe ab utna overwhelming nahi lag raha. I can take these small steps and try to survive without completely losing my sanity in this new environment.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledge her resilience and closure.
text: Mujhe yeh sun kar bohot sukoon mila ki tum aage ke samay ke liye in chhote-chhote steps ko shuru karne ka hausla juta paayi aur apna bharosa wapas la rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Log kya kahenge agar NRI beti ne ghar ki zimmedariyon se moonh mod liya, bas yahi soch kar chup rehti hoon
text: Log kya kahenge agar NRI beti ne ghar ki zimmedariyon se moonh mod liya, bas yahi soch kar chup rehti hoon. Studies ke saath shaadi ka dabaav aur caregiving ne mujhe itna nichod diya hai ki main dost ke function mein nahi ja paayi, par maine koi shikayat nahi ki. Main nahi chahti ki ghar walon ko lage ki shehar badalne se mera svabhav badal gaya hai, isliye main apni health neglect karke bas unki khushi ka dhyan rakhti hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational element of protecting family image by burying her exhaustion.
text: Tum apne ghar walon ki khushi aur unke is bharose ko banaye rakhne ke liye, apne andar ki is gehri thakan aur neglect ko akele hi daba rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, kyunki agar maine thodi bhi limit set ki, toh turant relatives bolenge ki 'bahar jaake iske par nikal aaye hain'. I don't want to seem selfish ya ungrateful. Mujhe unki care karni hai, but meri khud ki energy absolutely zero ho chuki hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feared consequence of being judged as selfish in response to the stigma cue.
text: Ek darr sa rehta hai ki tumhari yeh genuine takleef aur exhaustion unhe tumhari khudgarzi ya 'badlaav' ki tarah lagegi...

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. Mummy ki tabiyat frequent kharab rehti hai, toh unka dhyaan rakhna padta hai. Upar se constantly rishton ki baatein aur ghar ke kaam. My studies are suffering badly. I just feel so drained out right now.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time and duration of carrying this dual burden constantly.
text: Tumhare din ke itne saare ghante sirf dusron ki care aur in expectations ko poora karne mein guzar rahe hain, jiski wajah se tumhari padhai aur energy poori tarah nichod rahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: It's a 24/7 cycle. Aur yeh shaadi ka dabaav toh sabse zyada suffocating hai. They want to settle me down immediately kyunki I am considered an 'NRI match'. I can't even tell them ki I need some time to just breathe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about how she copes with the pressure of being treated as an NRI match.
text: Jab tum par is tarah se bina tumhari marzi ke shaadi ka yeh lagatar pressure banta hai, toh in sab baaton ke beech tum khud ko kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Main bas muskura deti hoon aur topic change kar deti hoon jab parents ke saamne hoti hoon. Par room mein aakar chupke se rona aata hai. I feel like main ek project ban gayi hoon, insaan nahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the duty frame of being treated as a project rather than a person.
text: Aise mahaul mein jahan tum khud ko sirf ek family 'project' aur zimmedari ki tarah dekhi ja rahi ho, apni asli needs ko dabana ek bohot bhari bojh ban gaya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: It really is so heavy. Main kisi aur ki life jee rahi hoon yahan. I miss the independence I had when I was studying abroad. Yahan aakar I am just totally trapped in this 'adarsh beti' box.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feeling of being trapped and helpless in this situation.
text: Lagta hai in sab parivaarik ummeedon aur apni azaadi khone ke is safar mein, tum poori tarah se fasa hua aur helpless mehsoos kar rahi ho, jahan aage ka koi rasta nahi dikh raha.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Log kya kahenge agar NRI beti ne ghar ki zimmedariyon se moonh mod liya, bas yahi soch kar chup rehti hoon
text: Helpless hi toh hoon. Mummy ki health kharab hai, aur main bas yeh sochti hoon ki log kya kahenge agar NRI beti ne ghar ki zimmedariyon se moonh mod liya, bas yahi soch kar chup rehti hoon. I can't break their hearts.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her struggle as a structural issue for returning NRIs, separating it from her worth.
text: Ek returning young aurat ke liye ghar aakar achanak se apni purani life aur azaadi chhodkar in bhari expectations mein fit hona bahut exhausting hota hai; yeh bilkul natural struggle hai aur isme tumhari koi kamzori nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thanks for saying that. Sometimes I just wish log mujhe thodi grace dein. Par NRI families mein expectations bohot rigid hoti hain. Sab kuch is society ke dikhawe pe chalta hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a systemic anecdote about NRI young women facing pressure.
text: NRI parivaaron mein ladkiyon pe society ke dar se jaldi settle hone aur har zimmedari chup-chap uthane ka bojh itna aam hai—sirf tumhara akele ka nahi, lekin koi is ghutan ko openly bolta nahi hai kyunki dikhawa zaroori samjha jata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes! Koi nahi bolta. Har koi bas ek perfect picture paint karna chahta hai social media pe aur relatives ke saamne. Main bhi wahi kar rahi hoon, and it is killing me inside. Ab toh mujhe constantly panic lagta hai health ko lekar.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Softly mirror the feared consequence of this facade causing her internal panic (stigma response).
text: Bahar se sab kuch ekdum perfect dikhane ki is lagatar majboori ne tumhare andar us panic aur takleef ko bohot bada kar diya hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul bada kar diya hai. I genuinely feel sick nowadays. Thakan itni hai ki classes aur assignments nahi ho pa rahe hain. I don't know how long I can keep this up before I completely crash.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about the fear of crashing completely.
text: Kya tumhe is baat ka sabse zyada darr lagta hai ki in saari family duties aur society ke pressure ko uthate hue tumhara apna future aur health hamesha ke liye bikhar jayenge?

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, career aur health hi toh sab kuch tha mera. Ab toh bas 'achi shaadi' unka goal hai. I want to save myself, lekin theek hona chahti hoon, batao main kya karoon in sab ke beech?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide 2 simple options fitting her routine to create a boundary.
text: Hum ek ya do aisi chhoti azaadiyaan try kar sakte hain jo tumhari kisi family duty ko disturb na karein. Ya toh din mein kaam karte waqt 15 minute earphones lagakar apne course ki koi audio suno, ya raat ko sone se pehle 10 minute bina phone ke akele balcony mein baitho. Inme se tumhare liye kya doable lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Balcony wala option sounds more peaceful. Din bhar kaam ke time padhai ki cheezein sunungi toh shayad aur overwhelm ho jaungi. Raat mein akele baithna will be a nice reset.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk her through the execution of the chosen step.
text: Chalo ise ek chote step se shuru karte hain: aaj raat sabke sone ke baad, apna phone room mein chhod kar sirf 10 minute balcony ki hawa mein akele jaakar baithna... bas itna hi aaj ke liye apni health ke khatir try karna.

speaker: Seeker
text: I think I can easily do that. Uss time mummy papa already so jaate hain. Koi mujhse dawai ya madad nahi maangega, aur na hi shaadi ki baat hogi. It will just be my time.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframe taking time as a necessity, not selfishness.
text: Ghar ki in endless zimmedariyon aur dikhawe ke beech apne liye aisi chhoti azaadi nikalna selfish nahi hota, balki aisi bhaari expectations waale mahaul mein apni sanity ko zinda rakhne ka zaroori tareeqa hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Log kya kahenge agar NRI beti ne ghar ki zimmedariyon se moonh mod liya, bas yahi soch kar chup rehti hoon
text: I really needed to hear that it's not selfish. Hamesha guilt aata hai, aur dimaag mein bas yahi chalta hai ki log kya kahenge agar NRI beti ne ghar ki zimmedariyon se moonh mod liya, bas yahi soch kar chup rehti hoon pichle kai mahino se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about how she will comfort herself in that safe 10 mins.
text: Jab tum aaj raat us akele aur shaant waqt mein balcony mein khadi hogi, toh in logon ke taano ke beech apne aap ko kya keh kar thoda sahara dena chahogi?

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad main khud ko bolungi ki 'you are doing your best, it's okay'. Main hamesha apne aap ko hi criticize karti rehti hoon ki main padhai mein focus nahi kar paa rahi despite their sacrifices.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how offering herself compassion will counter the duty frame.
text: Dusron ki zimmedariyon mein khud ko galat thehrane ki aadat se hatkar, ab jab tum apne aap ko yeh samajh aur grace dogi, toh us duty ka bojh shayad andar se thoda halka ho jayega.

speaker: Seeker
text: I really hope so. Kyunki perfection ka bojh bahut bhari hai. Caregiving mein perfect daughter, career mein achi NRI ladki, aur society mein marriage material. It's totally impossible to be all that at once.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time/duration of this exhausting impossible task.
text: Har roz aur har pal is namumkin perfection ko banaye rakhne ki iss koshish ne hi tumhari saari energy ko itne samay se lagatar nichod kar rakh diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. Nichod hi diya hai mujhe. But jab main aaj raat uss balcony mein rahungi, I will consciously leave that 'perfect' girl inside the room. Bahar sirf main hungi, without any labels.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about the feeling of being without labels.
text: Jab tum us 'perfect' ladki aur un saare labels ko room ke andar chhod kar akele khule aasmaan ke neeche hogi, toh us azaadi mein kaisa mehsoos hone ki ummeed kar rahi ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad thodi shanti milegi ek break ki tarah. Ek temporary pause button. No 'log kya kahenge', no shaadi pressure, no caregiving guilt. Just absolute silence aur meri apni thoughts.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her finding a way to connect back to her core self without duties.
text: Woh shanti bhara temporary pause button tumhare is bhari family routine ke beech, tumhe wapas apne asal wajood aur azaadi se judne ka ek rasta dega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes. And I really needed to realize this. Thank you for not judging me. Mere aas-paas sabhi logon ko lagta hai mujhse bas chup chap sab sehne ki umeed karni chahiye. Yeh conversation bahut comforting thi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reiterate that the expectation of silent suffering is a structural flaw.
text: Society aur biradari ka yeh structure aisa hi banaya gaya hai jahan ladkiyon ki complaints ko nakara jata hai, isliye is pure situation mein tumhari thakan puri tarah se valid hai aur yeh expectation unki problem hai, tumhari nahi.

speaker: Seeker
text: I understand now. Mujhe apne is chote se boundary ko hamesha defend karna hai, even if mentally initially. Aaj raat se main pakka apna 'pause button' try karungi. I feel emotionally lighter now.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Warmly acknowledge her readiness and the boundary she has set.
text: Mujhe yeh sun kar bahut sukoon mil raha hai ki tum khud ko in endless zimmedariyon ke beech ek zaroori saans lene ki jagah dene ke is ahem kadam ke liye puri tarah taiyar ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe
text: Wapas India aakar lagta hai jaise main apni hi zameen par ek ajnabi hoon, par ye baat main Mummy ko nahi bol sakti kyunki wo pehle hi meri safety ko lekar pareshan rehti hain. Shaadi na ja paane ka dukh maine daba liya taaki ghar mein koi naya tamasha na ho aur Papa ka mujh par bharosa bana rahe. Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational element of protecting parents' peace and avoiding family conflict.
text: Tum apne parents ki pareshani na badhane aur ghar ki izzat aur shanti bachane ke liye, apne is naye aur anjaan mehsus karne wale ehsaas ko andar hi andar daba rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, kyunki agar maine kaha ki mujhe adjust karne mein problem ho rahi hai, toh sab bolenge ki bahar rehkar mere 'nakhre' badh gaye hain. Koi nahi samjhega. Sabko lagta hai India wapas aana sabse easy hona chahiye.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feared consequence softly in response to the stigma cue.
text: Is darr ne tumhe rok rakha hai ki log tumhare is struggle ko samajhne ke bajaye, usay tumhara attitude ya nakhre samajh kar judge karenge...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe
text: Exactly. Koi isey asli problem manta hi nahi. Jab friends ki shaadi bhi miss ki, toh maine bas thakan ka bahana de diya. Main apne return shock ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki harmony bani rahe, but sach mein I feel so disconnected from everything here.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask an open-ended question about her feeling of disconnection.
text: Jab apne hi ghar mein aur apno ke beech hote hue bhi andar se yeh disconnect ka ehsaas bahut gehra hota hai, toh tum khud ko is ajnabipan se kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Main bas smile karti rehti hoon poora din. Room mein aakar apni purani photos scroll karti hoon jab main bahar thi. I miss that freedom so much. Yahan aakar toh main bas ek obedient beti ban gayi hoon jiski har cheez par sabki nazar hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the role frame of becoming an obedient daughter and losing freedom.
text: Ek azaad life jeene ke baad, ab achanak se sabki ummeedon ke mutabik 'achi beti' ka role nibhana tumhari apni identity ko bohot chhota kar raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe
text: Chhota nahi, khatam kar diya hai. Mujhe lagta tha wapas aakar main relax karungi, but yahan ka culture shock mujhe mar raha hai. Main kisise share bhi nahi kar sakti warna naya tamasha ban jayega.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the feeling of being trapped and helpless due to relational constraints.
text: Lagta hai is naye badlaav aur chup rehne ki majboori ne tumhe bilkul trapped aur helpless kar diya hai, jahan tumhara apna koi azaad rasta nahi dikh raha.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, helpless hi hoon. Mujhe lagta hai main unke pyar aur sacrifices ke bojh ke neeche dab gayi hoon. I love my parents, par iska matlab ye nahi ki main saans hi na loon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her struggle as a common acculturation issue for returning young women, separating it from her worth as a daughter.
text: Tumhara yeh culture shock mehsoos karna bilkul natural hai; jab koi wapas ek aise samaj mein aata hai jahan parivaar ki izzat pehle aati hai, toh adjust karna bahut exhausting hota hai, aur is struggle ka matlab ye nahi ki tum buri beti ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you. Yeh sunkar sach mein halka lag raha hai. Mujhe hamesha lagta tha ki the problem is with me. Ki maybe main hi adapt nahi kar pa rahi hoon aur selfish ho gayi hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a systemic anecdote to normalize her experience of return shock.
text: Young returning auraton mein yeh achanak aayi boundaries aur space kho dene ka ehsaas bohot common hai — sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki society lagatar yaad dilati hai ki humein shukraguzar hona chahiye.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe
text: Shukraguzar wali baat itni sach hai. Parents ne mere liye sab kuch kiya hai. Unki izzat ke liye main kuch bhi karungi. Par kabhi kabhi mann karta hai bas chillaun aur kahu ki please give me some space.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Explore what 'space' looks like to her.
text: Jab andar se aise chillane aur thodi 'space' mangne ki itni tez zaroorat mehsoos hoti hai, toh tumhare khayalon mein woh safe aur azaad space kaisi dikhti hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Mere khayalon mein aisi space hai jahan mujhe baar-baar explain na karna pade ki main kahan ja rahi hoon ya phone par kyun hoon. Bas thoda akelapan jahan koi log kya kahenge wala drama na ho. Ab kya karoon in sab ke beech theek rehne ke liye?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide 2 simple options fitting her routine to create a boundary without causing 'tamasha'.
text: Hum do chhoti cheezein try kar sakte hain jinse koi naya kalesh na ho. Ya toh tum din mein ek baar kisi kaam ya course ka naam lekar 20 minute headphones lagakar apne room ka door lock karlo, ya phir dopahar mein apni favourite kitaab lekar balcony mein akele baitho. In dono mein se kaun sa aasaan lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Headphones wala option better hai. Agar main room lock karungi aur headphones honge toh mummy ko lagega koi important online meeting chal rahi hai. They won't disturb me then.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk her through the execution of the chosen step.
text: Pehle kal din mein apne room mein jaana... phir room lock karke aur headphones lagakar apna favourite music ya bas shanti enjoy karna... bas itna hi aaj ke liye apni space ke liye try karna.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe
text: Haan, yeh doable hai. Isme kisi ki feelings hurt nahi hongi. Main unhe disappoint nahi kar sakti kyunki mujhe unki health ka darr rehta hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframe taking time as a necessity for sanity, not selfishness.
text: Aisi chhoti spaces nikalna humare jaise samaj mein bahut zaruri hota hai jahan azaadi kam hoti hai; yeh unhe disappoint karna nahi hai, balki lambe samay tak apni mental health maintain karne ka tareeqa hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: True. Agar main andar se healthy nahi rahungi toh main kisi ke liye 'achi beti' nahi ban paungi long term mein. Par yeh constantly act karna bahut exhausting hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time and duration of the exhausting act.
text: Lamba waqt sirf auron ki khushi ke hisaab se jeene ka yeh natak karte rehna waqai poori tarah thaka dene wala hota hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes. Bahut zyada thaka dene wala. Main jab kal headphones lagaungi, I won't listen to music. Main shayad thoda journal karungi us 20 minute mein. It might help me process this alien feeling.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about how journaling will help her in that space.
text: Jab tum akele us 20 minute mein bina kisi darr ke likhna shuru karogi, toh us journaling ke zariye apni iss thakan ko kaise halka karne ka soch rahi ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad main openly wahan ye accept kar saku ki mujhse galti ho gayi wapas aakar. Yahan toh galti bolne par bhi izzat ka issue ban jayega. Us journal mein kam se kam main sach toh bol sakti hoon apne aap se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her finding a safe way to accept her truth without the family pressure.
text: Logon ki nazron aur izzat girne ke darr se door, us journal mein apne aap ko sach bolne ki azaadi dena tumhara apne liye ek bahut sahara ban raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul sahara hai. Main soch rahi hoon usme apni purani life aur yahan ki reality ka ek honest comparison likhungi. Un filtered. Mujhe apne thoughts ko validate karne ki zaroorat hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her active step to process the acculturation disconnect.
text: Apne in do alag duniya ke anubhav ko bina kisi family filter ke likhna tumhare uss deep disconnect ko process karne ka ek zaroori zariya ban gaya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes it has. It will give me proof that my feelings are real and not just me throwing an 'NRI tantrum'. Papa Mummy ki expectations meet karna ek alag struggle hai, par khud se jhooth bolna sabse zyada painful tha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalize that acknowledging the pain does not negate her love for her family.
text: Apne is safar aur takleef ko sach maan kar apni hi nazron mein sahi theherana, kisi bhi tarah se parivaar se tumhare pyar ko kam nahi karta, balki khud ko zinda rakhne ke liye ek samajhdaari hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Main apne return shock aur depression ka 'natak' nahi karna chahti taaki parivaar ki 'izzat' aur harmony bani rahe
text: I really appreciate you saying that. Main apne return shock aur depression ka natak nahi karti the par main apni suffering ka 'theek hone' wala natak bhi ab nahi karna chahti, at least khud ke samne toh nahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her resolution to stop pretending with herself.
text: Logon ke samne us perfect duty aur harmony wale natak ko beshak nibhao, par akele mein ab apne hi dard ko jhuthlane wala kaam tum khud se hatane ko taiyar ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes exactly. Pura natak ek sath chhodungi toh waise bhi mummy ki health par asar padega. I'll take baby steps. Akele room ka 20 minute journaling is my first baby step towards healing.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about how she feels taking this first baby step.
text: Jab tum mummy ki health ka dhyan rakhte hue bhi apne liye yeh zaroori baby step uthane ke baare mein soch rahi ho, toh abhi kaisa mehsoos ho raha hai andar se?

speaker: Seeker
text: I feel a bit more in control. Pehle main poori tarah overwhelmed thi. Ab lag raha hai ki a slowly I can process this massive change in my life without blowing up everything around me.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her feeling of regaining control over the overwhelming acculturation stress.
text: Woh jo darr tha ki sab kuch toot jayega ya bura ho jayega, wo ab thoda shant ho gaya hai aur tumhe apne haalat par thoda aur control lag raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes, bilkul. The fear is manageable now. I just needed to say it out loud without someone rushing in to tell me how lucky I am to be back home. Thank you for listening to me patiently.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Warmly acknowledge her readiness and the step she took.
text: Mujhe yeh sun kar bahut achha lag raha hai ki tum itne waqt ke apne akelepan aur darr se bahar nikal kar in nayi koshishon ki taraf aage badhne ke liye puri tarah taiyar ho.
